spec_match.rationale: Walks `navigator.clipboard.read()` inside the clipboardchange handler, iterates ClipboardItem.types and updates UI per MIME — the canonical multi-format pattern.

interactive.rationale: Three seed buttons (plain / HTML / image), live chip array that flips state on every event, plus a content preview pane.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises MIME type chips flipping live; the page does exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers three MIME types out of the box and surfaces `web text/uri-list` as a chip, but never lights it up because the seed buttons don't write it. Custom format support is the chrome 140 chrome-status callout.

references.rationale: Links the W3C spec but doesn't link the chromestatus URL on this concept — only the feature index has it. The see-also should include both.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, chip-active state uses --accent-emerald, no static card.

openQuestions.title: Demo a custom `web ` format
openQuestions.detail: The 'web text/uri-list' chip is rendered but never activated by any seed button. Either drop it or add a seed that writes a custom format (`web text/figma-frame` style) using ClipboardItem with a `web ` prefix — that's the unsanitized format support that v140 unlocks alongside clipboardchange.

openQuestions.title: Show image dimensions in the preview
openQuestions.detail: Image preview currently shows MIME and byte size. Decoding the blob and rendering the actual image (or at least width × height) would prove the read returned real bytes, not a placeholder.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page is supposed to cite the chromestatus URL — it's the recovery anchor when slugs get mangled (per CLAUDE.md invariant 3). This page links the W3C spec but not chromestatus.

summary: Clean multi-format demo, but it rendered a chip it doesn't drive (`web text/uri-list`) and forgot the chromestatus link. Add a real custom-format seed and image preview to make the spec coverage credible.
